evidence: CLI/multivol/api_strings_extension.py has NO imports, references undefined `app` and `DB_PATH`, and contains route logic (get_strings_content, download_strings) that was fully migrated into CLI/multivol/api_server/routes/scan.py as Blueprint routes. The file is not imported anywhere in the codebase.

CLI/multivol/api_server/database.py CREATE TABLE still includes `filepath TEXT` (line 20) alongside `dump_path TEXT` (line 21). New database installations will have a dead `filepath` column; the migration shim at lines 79-83 that copies filepath->dump_path only applies to pre-migration databases.

CLI/multivol/api_server/utils.py resolve_host_path() contains a labeled fallback: `# Fallback to the old storage hack just in case` (line 28-33) that checks for `'storage'` in the container path — a compatibility shim for an older path convention.

CLI/multivol/multivol.py line 158: `# TODO: Add wrapper for Vol2 or update vol2 logic.` — the progress-bar wrapper (vol3_wrapper) is only wired for vol3; vol2 parallel execution is missing analogous progress integration.
database.py migration logic has 4 in-place ALTER TABLE shims (dump_path, config_json, case_name->name, mode) that run every startup — these are working but represent visible schema evolution debt.
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: api_strings_extension.py is a dead pre-migration fragment that was never deleted after route logic was moved into the Blueprint system; the `filepath` ghost column in CREATE TABLE will be present in all fresh installations.

strengths: The vol2/vol3 split is intentional and cleanly separated into distinct modules (multi_volatility2.py, multi_volatility3.py) with no cross-contamination of their APIs.
Database schema migration shims in init_db() are explicit and well-commented, logging each alteration — a developer can trace the schema's history.
Active code uses only the current `dump_path` column name consistently; no production query path reads the legacy `filepath` column.
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The issues are isolated legacy artifacts left behind after two migrations (routes refactored into Blueprints, schema field renamed from filepath to dump_path and case_name to name) rather than a systemic pattern of old-and-new APIs coexisting in the same call paths.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase has a handful of concrete migration artifacts: an orphaned pre-Blueprint file (api_strings_extension.py) that is unreachable dead code, a ghost `filepath` column in the CREATE TABLE, and a labeled legacy shim in resolve_host_path. These are real incomplete-migration smells but they do not represent competing API patterns or deprecated symbols actively called by production code — they are inert leftovers. The codebase sits comfortably above the 60 'significant drag' anchor because no developer is confused by dual APIs in a live call path, but the dead file and ghost column keep it well below 85.

issues.summary: api_strings_extension.py is a pre-Blueprint migration fragment, unreachable and referencing undefined names
evidence: File starts directly with `@app.route(...)` with no imports — `app`, `DB_PATH`, `sqlite3`, `send_file`, `jsonify`, `request` are all undefined within the file.
scan.py Blueprint at lines 900-981 contains identical route logic for `/results/<uuid>/strings` and `/results/<uuid>/strings/download` as proper registered Blueprint routes.
No import of api_strings_extension anywhere in the codebase (grep confirms zero references).
issues.suggestion: Delete CLI/multivol/api_strings_extension.py entirely — its functionality already lives in scan.py as scan_bp routes.

issues.summary: CREATE TABLE still declares deprecated `filepath` column alongside its replacement `dump_path`
evidence: database.py line 20: `filepath TEXT,` still present in CREATE TABLE scans, while line 21 has `dump_path TEXT`.
Migration shim at lines 79-83 copies `filepath` -> `dump_path` for old databases, but for new installations both columns are created — `filepath` will always be NULL/empty.
No application code reads `scan['filepath']` anywhere; column is dead on fresh installs.
issues.suggestion: Remove `filepath TEXT,` from the CREATE TABLE definition. The migration shim (lines 76-83) can remain for upgrading existing databases but should check only for `dump_path` absence.

issues.summary: resolve_host_path contains labeled 'old storage hack' fallback for a deprecated path layout
evidence: utils.py lines 28-33: `# Fallback to the old storage hack just in case` block checks `if 'storage' in container_path` and reconstructs paths via `container_path.find('storage')` — explicit acknowledgment this is legacy.
The primary path translation logic using BASE_DIR precedes this block; the fallback is only reached when the new logic fails, suggesting it covers infrastructure setups that no longer exist.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'old storage hack' fallback block (lines 28-33) once confident that all deployments use the current BASE_DIR-relative path convention. Add a warning log instead of silently falling through.

root_causes: Blueprint refactor cleanup was incomplete: the source snippet file was not deleted after its logic was moved into scan_bp.
Schema migration was additive (ALTER TABLE) but the CREATE TABLE template was never updated to remove the deprecated column, leaving new-install schemas carrying dead fields.
